title: THE WEBSITE THAT DOESN'T EXIST (April Fools, 2026)
description: A self-aware April Fools product-launch parody that proudly announces itself as fake, complete with confetti, jester hats, and a pink moon.

title: 244,036 Miles Away
description: A breathing Pink Moon meditation — one glowing orb, the distance to tonight's full moon, and a quiet place to exhale amidst the noise.

title: Flow State — Wednesday, April 1
description: A generative particle flow-field that paints itself endlessly in earthy spring pigments, quietly noting the strangeness of today.

title: Mondfahrt (Moonride)
description: A kaleidoscopic, real-time countdown experience celebrating humanity's return to the moon after 54 years, with pulsing typography and orbital mechanics.

title: Das Danach (The After)
description: A quiet meditation on the day after Artemis launched—footprints on a grey lunar surface, the soft thrum of distance, and the weight of human ambition now hurtling through the void.

title: Vier Punkte über der Erde (Four Dots Above the Earth)
description: A minimal real-time visualization celebrating humanity's return to lunar orbit—four glowing points representing the Artemis II crew, orbiting through an abstract night sky while their mission metrics stream below.

title: Perspective — The View That Changes Everything
description: An immersive, full-screen meditation on scale and perspective—Earth shrinking away as the crew's viewpoint shifts from terrestrial to celestial, with ephemeral moments of light and distance revealing the profound smallness of home.

title: Two Worlds, One Friday — April 3, 2026
description: A split-screen living front page for Good Friday 2026 — the top half a serene, animated cosmos tracking Orion's journey moonward, the bottom half a scrolling ticker of earthly headlines, divided by a luminous horizon line that breathes between them.

title: Signal · Holy Saturday · Flight Day 4
description: A CRT terminal receiving a live transmission log from Orion Integrity on Flight Day 4 — crew dispatches from deep space interleaved with fragmented news from a burning world below, rendered in phosphor-green on black with scanline overlay and a blinking cursor.

title: Earthrise — Easter · Flight Day 5
description: A living canvas where a crescent Earth and a rising Moon drift through a hand-painted cosmos — the whole screen breathing with Easter light on the morning Orion is one day from its closest approach.

title: Two Frequencies — The Day the Moon Was Close
description: A real-time dual-signal oscilloscope canvas — one waveform for the heavens (Artemis II at closest lunar approach today), one for the earth below (war, deadlines, fire), the two signals occasionally merging and repelling, with live mission milestones ticking down the side.

title: Coming Home — Flight Day 7
description: A meditative star-field where four glowing crew-dots drift slowly earthward through an ink-black void, while fragments of today's world — war, wonder, World Health Day — drift past as fading comets.

title: Two Silences — Wednesday, April 8, 2026
description: A dual-channel transmission board — one frequency for Orion hurtling home through the dark, one for a world that just pulled back from the edge — rendered as living signal-paper with handwritten annotations, burning phosphor lines, and a shared countdown to Friday.

title: The Fragile Planet Gazette — Flight Day 9
description: A living broadsheet newspaper printed in deep space — ink bleeding like cosmic radiation, headlines drifting home with the crew, every column a different frequency of the same urgent beautiful world.

title: Signal Recovered — The Deep Returns
description: A bioluminescent deep-sea sonar interface marking the moment Artemis II touched the Pacific — four humans retrieved from the void — while the world's frequencies crackle on every channel.

title: Yuri's Night, 65 — The Long Arc
description: A warm typographic broadside for April 12 — Yuri's Night, 65 years after Gagarin's orbit — rendered as a hand-printed celestial poster with constellation geometry, live clock, and today's world dispatches inked in column type.
